$comment: STANDALONE walkthrough — Level 4 never existed in 2001 and is deliberately kept OUT of the canon donnie.steps.json run. This slice enters the level directly; on the live site it is reached from the L3 ending. UPDATED 2026-06-05 for the design-system/Level 4 redesign (new flow: collapse → letter → two-phase hunt [obituary ring → /news/pop7.html popup, dismissing it reveals the door mark] → whisper → frank → door) — NOT YET RE-RUN headless; the ending reference still (l4-ending-reference.png) is from the old design and must be recaptured before restoring the match-reference assert. All navigates carry ?seed=42 so the sparrow hotspot — and therefore the screenshots — are deterministic; without a seed the hunt is truly random.

steps.caption: October 2, 1988 — the morning the engine should have fallen. Over a pixelated still of the jet engine, words assemble: “the tangent universe collapsed. everything you did is undone. and yet — you remember.” A red .continue. fades in.
steps.note: Assembly: 700ms hold + 19 words × 150ms ≈ 3.6s, then the 0.6s continue fade. diffThreshold tightened: a single assembling word is ~0.1% of the stage.

steps.caption: Click .continue. and the void gives way to paper — the Primary Universe, restored.

Roberta Sparrow’s letter types in, line by line, an ink caret tracking the active line: “the world did not end. you unmade it… the dead wait behind a door you have opened before.” The way onward hides in her own words — “say them where the steps go down to the dark.”
steps.note: Typing: 650ms hold + 13 lines × 360ms ≈ 5.3s, key goes live ~450ms later. Fixed ceiling is safe — the content is deterministic; the next step's waitVisibleMs gates on the key being clickable.

steps.caption: The phrase “go down to the dark” is the door. Click it.

Near-total darkness: “her name was that of a bird.” One faint mark in the dark — a ring, the shape the obituaries wore back in Level 1. It opens the morning paper in a new tab: Middlesex Times-Dispatch, October 6, 1988 — DARKO, DONALD J. (1972-1988). In the restored universe the engine fell, and you were under it. Read it; close it.
steps.note: The hunt is TWO-PHASE (L1's grammar: obituaries first, then the way on). Only the ring (#obit) exists at first — the door mark (#mark) is `hidden` until the popup tab is DISMISSED (the page intercepts the click, window.opens /news/pop7.html, and polls w.closed @400ms → reveals #mark). The runner captures + auto-closes the popup, which IS the dismissal. Both marks share ONE seeded PRNG stream (mulberry32(42)): #mark consumes draws 1-2 (so its seed-42 position is unchanged from before the ring existed), #obit draws 3-4 and redraws until ≥140 stage-px clear of the door. Popup-blocked fallback reveals the door anyway (don't brick the level).

steps.caption: Put the paper down and a second mark fades into the dark — a cross this time, somewhere else, placed RANDOMLY on every visit. The walkthrough pins it with a fixed seed; a human has to hunt twice. Find it and click: it is the door.
steps.note: placeHotspot derives the position from ?seed=42 (mulberry32) within 12–82% x / 16–78% y; without a seed it is Math.random per load. #mark only becomes visible after l4-obituary's popup closes (runner auto-close → w.closed poll → reveal + fade) — waitVisibleMs covers that handoff. domClick targets the element by selector, so the click lands wherever the seed put it. No navigate: re-loading would re-hide the mark behind the ring gate.

steps.caption: A single Win98 window on the void — “whisper”. say the words. No red anywhere: the only screen with no signpost. The level has taught you the words; here you must already know them.
Type the words: cellar door.
steps.note: Deliberately does NOT press Enter — the typed words get their own frame; the next step submits. Wrong words escalate through three native alerts (THOSE ARE NOT THE WORDS / TRY TO REMEMBER / SHE TOLD YOU ONCE); the close box refuses.

steps.caption: Press Enter. Say them out loud, if you like — the site can’t hear you, but the door can.
steps.note: Focus is still in the #pw input from the previous domType; Enter submits (wrong words alert instead of navigating).

steps.caption: FRANK, in a red-barred window over his pixelated mask: “WAKE UP, DONNIE. I HAVE BEEN WATCHING.” The 28-day count fades in and drains to zero on an accelerating curve — time collapsing inward. At zero it glitches, the verdict lands — “THAT IS WHEN THE WORLD ENDS. YOU ARE OUT OF TIME.” — and .open the door. appears.
steps.note: Wall-clock beat: voice ≈ 650ms + 7×130ms, drain starts ~2.1s, runs 5.2s, door reveals ~8.7s. State is derived from elapsed time, so the door is always reached.

steps.caption: “.open the door.” — Frank’s last words are the only way out. Click.

The cellar door, open — chalkboard light sinking into the void, the words held bottom-left: “you said the words. the door is open.” No red, and nowhere left to click. The signpost is spent because the journey is over.
steps.note: TEMPORARY diff-stable: the old l4-ending-reference.png predates the redesign. Recapture the reference from a verified seed=42 run, then restore: { "mode": "match-reference", "reference": "../assets/l4-ending-reference.png", "matchThreshold": 0.12, "diffThreshold": 0.0008, "minWaitMs": 8000, "maxWaitMs": 30000, "pollIntervalMs": 1000 }.
